slides.title: Python's Secret Weapon: Libraries
slides.body: Python comes with thousands of **free, ready-made libraries** — so you don't have to build everything from scratch. Need to fetch data, draw charts, or train an AI? There's a library for that.
slides.tip: Install any library instantly: pip install library-name

slides.title: Analyse Data With Pandas
slides.body: **Pandas** gives you a `DataFrame` — a powerful table with rows and columns, like Excel in Python. Filter, sort, and calculate stats in just a few lines.
slides.code:
import pandas as pd

df = pd.DataFrame({"name": ["Alice", "Bob"], "score": [88, 95]})
print(df["score"].mean())

slides.title: Talk to the Web With Requests
slides.body: **Requests** lets your Python code fetch data from any URL — just like a browser does. Get a JSON response from an API in two lines.
slides.tip: 200 means success — you got the data!

slides.title: Test Your Code With pytest
slides.body: **pytest** automatically finds and runs any function that starts with `test_`, then gives you a clear pass/fail report. Write tests, run one command, sleep soundly.

slides.title: Pick the Right Library
slides.body: Every task has a go-to library: **Pandas** for data, **Matplotlib** for charts, **Requests** for APIs, **Flask** for web apps, **scikit-learn** for ML. You now know where to look — the ecosystem is yours to explore.
slides.tip: Install only what you need, and use a virtual environment for each project.
